name: gmrepo

relevance.justification: GMrepo v3 satisfies two of three primary criteria strongly: (1) species-level microbiome data — 87,048 16S rRNA samples (ASV→species via Greengenes 13.8) plus 31,917 WGS samples (MetaPhlAn2); (2) rich phenotypic/disease data — 302 diseases across 118,965 samples. Demographic completeness is the limiting factor: the v2 paper explicitly flagged that ~30% of samples lack age/gender/BMI even after manual curation, and the v3 summary confirms sex annotation is sparse (~30% coverage). Per-sample demographics (age, sex, BMI) are present but incomplete across the corpus, preventing a score of 9. Additional criteria met: fully structured SQL/tabular format, actively maintained (v3 Jan 2026), NCBI Taxonomy + NCBI MeSH identifiers used.

novelty.justification: No BioThings API covers gut microbiome abundance data with disease-marker annotations. GMrepo adds entity types (runs/samples, taxa) and relations (taxa↔phenotype pair markers, cross-dataset marker consistency scores via MCI) entirely absent from MyChem, MyGene, MyDisease, MyVariant. WGS metagenomic data (MetaPhlAn2 species profiles) is an additional layer absent from PRIME. The disease-marker (LEfSe LDA scores) and Marker Consistency Index (MCI) annotations are novel structured data not available anywhere in BioThings. Complements rather than duplicates PRIME (gut-only vs multi-body-site; adds WGS; richer disease focus).

openness.justification: Licensed CC BY-NC 3.0 — non-commercial restriction applies. Per evaluation checklist, BioThings/Su Lab is a non-profit academic institution (Scripps Research), so NC restriction is generally compatible; flag for downstream commercial API consumers. Paper states 'freely accessible to all academic users' with no login required. REST API confirmed (no auth, no rate limits per documentation). SQL dump directory at /Downloads/SQLDumps/ returned HTTP 403 (directory listing blocked) — actual file downloads may still work with direct filenames. Score 3/5 for NC restriction + bulk download URL uncertainty.

evidence.scope: 118,965 human gut metagenome samples from 890 projects (v3, Jan 2026). 87,048 16S rRNA + 31,917 WGS samples. 302 diseases/phenotypes. Per-sample metadata: age, sex, BMI, country, diet, antibiotic usage (incomplete — ~30% missing demographics). Disease markers: 1,299 taxa (726 species, 573 genera) for 167 phenotype pairs from 275 projects. Marker Consistency Index (MCI) for reproducibility scoring across studies.
evidence.overlap: No overlap with existing BioThings APIs — gut microbiome abundance and disease-marker data is a new entity type. NCBI Taxonomy IDs link taxa to MyGene.info. NCBI MeSH disease terms could link to MyDisease.info. Partial conceptual overlap with PRIME (also 16S gut data) but GMrepo adds WGS layer, disease-marker annotations, and MCI scores; PRIME has fuller demographics and multi-body-site coverage.

license.name: CC BY-NC 3.0
restrictions: Non-commercial use only — flag for downstream commercial API consumers of BioThings

api_info.record_coverage: projects, runs, phenotypes, species/genus relative abundances, marker taxa, co-occurrence data

risks: Demographic incompleteness: ~30% of samples lack age/gender/BMI even after manual curation — limits demographic analysis coverage
CC BY-NC 3.0 restriction must be flagged to downstream commercial consumers of BioThings APIs
SQL dump bulk download directory returned HTTP 403 — verify individual file download URLs before ingestion (filenames documented at evolgeniusteam.github.io/gmrepodocumentation)
Gut-only scope: no multi-body-site data (complement with PRIME for broader body-site coverage)
WGS taxonomic profiles use older MetaPhlAn2 (v3 paper); 16S uses Greengenes 13.8 (outdated vs GG2 2024 used in PRIME)
v2 (evaluated article) is superseded by v3 (Jan 2026); ingest from v3 canonical URL
next_actions: Run datasource-site-inspection to verify individual SQL dump file URLs and confirm download accessibility
Prioritize: all_runs metadata file (demographics), relative_abundances at species level (16S and WGS separately)
Confirm NCBI Taxonomy IDs are present in abundance tables for entity linking
Note: evaluate alongside PRIME for complementary ingestion — GMrepo adds WGS + disease markers; PRIME adds multi-body-site + full demographics
Proceed to biothings-plugin-generator after site inspection confirms download URLs
notes: User submitted v2 article URL (NAR 50:D777, 2022); evaluation performed against current canonical release GMrepo v3 (NAR 54:D734, Jan 2026, doi:10.1093/nar/gkaf1190). v2 interface archived at https://gmrepo2022.humangut.info.
